concerns.text: Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) is marked PASS, but the plan does not explicitly describe the mechanism for the Reference-Validator Agent to verify citations against primary sources before they contribute to the study. The plan mentions 'Citations in research.md will strictly adhere...' but lacks the specific validation step required by the Constitution's 'Verified Accuracy Gate' (Section: Verified Accuracy Gate, Point 1 & 2).

Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) is marked PASS, but the plan fails to explicitly state that checksums for raw data files will be recorded in the project state file (`state/projects/...yaml`) as required by the Constitution ('Data Hygiene' section: 'Every file under data/ is checksummed in the project's state... artifact_hashes map'). The plan only mentions 'checksums' generally.

The plan states 'Sample of ~50 repositories' but does not explicitly link this to the Success Criteria SC-001 (which requires ≥10 PRs per repo) or the data model's `pr_count_12m` constraint. While implied, the plan should explicitly reference SC-001 to ensure the sample size logic is traceable to the spec's success criteria.

Constitution Principle V (Versioning Discipline) is marked PASS, but the plan does not explicitly mention the generation or updating of the project state file (`state/projects/...yaml`) with content hashes for *all* artifacts, only 'Content hashes will be recorded'. The Constitution requires this specific file update mechanism to invalidate stale review records.
